diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 79de093..c462284 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -240,2 +240,9 @@ Day 231 / Week 33 observations:
 - Open issues fell to 2,945, so emergency support spend can be trimmed. Capacity tier 6 remains appropriate while enterprise decisions are pending.
 - Week 33 adjustment: keep prices/quality/quotas stable, reduce global ops to $60k/day and S2 targeted ops to $100k/day, keep S2 dev and maintenance content spend, and post a reliability/capacity update.
+
+Day 238 / Week 34 observations:
+- Cash recovered to $2.00M despite tier-9 R&D, and individual subs crossed 100k. Cancellations stayed controlled at 566, though backlog rose from 2,945 to 3,497 after the support trim.
+- Enterprise leads still have not converted, but group insights show slow decision cycles: E2 averages 16.3 days and E3 23.8 days, so the first large cohorts may still be evaluating.
+- 66 new enterprise leads arrived with 32,911 seats. Sent the same lower introductory pricing as last week: E1 $7/$12/$20, E2 $9/$17/$28, E3 $12/$25/$42 for A/B/C.
+- Started tier-10 R&D. It rolled an unusually fast 30-day expected completion and large +1.7232 expected quality boost, making it worth the cash drawdown. Also started level-2 research for E1/E2/E3 to improve enterprise pricing accuracy.
+- Week 34 adjustment: keep tier-6 capacity and product settings stable, reduce global ops to $50k/day and S2 targeted ops to $75k/day to protect cash after tier-10, keep S2 dev and maintenance content spend, and post a roadmap/reliability update.
